target.name: Union
target.founder: Will Somerindyke, Jon Reiland, Joe Musselman

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $51M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Union is a Seed-stage defense and security company that has raised $51.3M to date. The broader Defense & Security sector comprises 10 peer companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities including Anduril Industries, Helsing, and Shield AI—though direct same-stage comparables are sparse within this narrow vertical.

Given the absence of sufficient same-stage peers in Defense & Security (0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage identified), the Peer-Median method is not applicable; relying on all-sector medians would introduce unwarranted noise. Stage-Progression analysis is therefore the primary applicable method. Using total capital raised ($51.3M) as a proxy for Seed-stage value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $128M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $109M to $147M (low to high), with a median of $128M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression midpoint but is derived from a single methodology. Key uncertainties include: the thinness of Defense & Security comparables at this stage, the appropriateness of a 2.5x multiplier given Union's specific risk profile and capital efficiency, and the absence of revenue or deployment metrics in the dataset. A more robust triangulation would require either additional same-stage private transactions or public market multiples calibrated to Union's growth trajectory.
